name: ProMIS Neurosciences Inc.
business: ProMIS Neurosciences develops antibody therapies and therapeutic vaccines for neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer's, multiple system atrophy, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, and other protein-misfolding diseases. The company's lead product candidate, PMN310, is a monoclonal antibody designed to selectively target toxic misfolded oligomers of amyloid-beta for Alzheimer's disease. The company also has preclinical candidates PMN267 for ALS and PMN442 for synucleinopathies, all discovered using its proprietary EpiSelect computational platform.

exposures.sentence: We are developing a pipeline of antibodies aimed at selectively targeting misfolded toxic forms of proteins that drive neurodegenerative diseases without interfering with the essential functions of the same properly folded proteins.

catalysts.event: Completion of six-month assessments in PRECISE-AD Phase 1b trial
catalysts.sentence: Based on current clinical trial patient visit schedules, the Company expects to complete the six-month assessments in the second quarter of 2026, with the blinded interim analysis anticipated in early third quarter 2026.

catalysts.event: Blinded interim analysis of PRECISE-AD Phase 1b trial
catalysts.sentence: Based on current clinical trial patient visit schedules, the Company expects to complete the six-month assessments in the second quarter of 2026, with the blinded interim analysis anticipated in early third quarter 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of dosing and all patient visits in PRECISE-AD Phase 1b trial
catalysts.sentence: Completion of dosing and all patient visits is expected in the fourth quarter of 2026, with top-line data anticipated in early 2027 following database lock and statistical analysis.

catalysts.event: Top-line data for PRECISE-AD Phase 1b trial
catalysts.sentence: Completion of dosing and all patient visits is expected in the fourth quarter of 2026, with top-line data anticipated in early 2027 following database lock and statistical analysis.
Enrollment in the PRECISE-AD study was completed in December 2025 with 144 subjects enrolled across 21 active sites in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: 6.9 million Americans aged 65+ living with Alzheimer's disease in 2024, projected to rise to 12.7 million by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: During 2024, it was estimated there were 6.9 million Americans 65 and older living with AD, and that number is projected to rise to 12.7 million by 2050 (www.alz.org, Alzheimer s disease Facts and Figures 2024).

tamClaims.claim: Alzheimer's and other dementias cost the U.S. $592 billion in 2023
tamClaims.sentence: In 2023, AD and other dementias cost the U.S. $592 billion, and those costs are projected to rise with the increasing number of patients.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 30,000 people in the US and 450,000 worldwide suffer from ALS, with 5,000 new cases annually in the US
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated there are currently 30,000 people in the United States and 450,000 people worldwide, suffering from ALS, with approximately 5,000 new cases arising in the United States annually.
